_comment: Deck source for docs/research/reduced-system-prompt-five-bug-study.md. Source only; no bundled HTML requested.

theme.name: rose-pine-moon
theme.fontFamily: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif

scenes.title: Reduced System Prompt Five-Bug Study
scenes.subtitle: Pilot evidence, honest limits, and the A/B run that would prove savings
scenes.narration: This deck turns the reduced system prompt research note into a short decision artifact: what we know from existing traces, what we do not know yet, and exactly how to run the five-bug A/B study without overclaiming.

scenes.lede: Does a focused replacement prompt reduce cost without lowering bug-fix quality?
scenes.body: The change under test is f97e340a: Claude gets Kitsoki's composed prompt through --system-prompt, Codex gets the same bytes through model_instructions_file, and the editable base lives at .kitsoki/system-prompt.md. The important comparison is same bug, same model, same oracle, only the prompt path changed.
scenes.narration: The research question is narrow. Does replacing the inherited harness prompt with Kitsoki's focused prompt reduce cost without lowering bug-fix quality? Same bugs, same model, same oracle. Only the system-prompt path changes.

scenes.title: What changed

cards.label: Template-aware
cards.sub: renders through the same prompt mechanism

scenes.narration: The implementation aligns the harnesses. Claude receives a replacement system prompt. Codex receives a model instructions file. The prompt is project-owned and template-rendered, so stories can control it transparently.

scenes.title: Pilot corpus: five unique bugs already traced
scenes.caption: No new live LLM calls were made. These are existing traces and cell reports.

scenes.title: Measured pilot usage
scenes.caption: Visible tokens are input + output + reasoning. Cache-read normalizes Codex cached_input_tokens and Claude cache_read_input_tokens.

scenes.lede: This is not a causal A/B result yet.
scenes.body: The existing traces were not run on both prompt variants. They prove the corpus is useful and that repeated context dominates cost, but they do not prove f97e340a saved money. A research-grade claim needs old-append-default versus focused-replace on the same five bugs.
scenes.narration: The limit matters. The existing traces were not run on both prompt variants. They justify the experiment, but they do not prove the new prompt saved money. For that, we need an A/B matrix.

scenes.title: Why the prompt lever is still worth testing

cards.label: System prefix repeats
cards.sub: one removed token can save many token-opportunities

cards.label: Less instruction conflict
cards.sub: focused operator contract, fewer inherited defaults

scenes.narration: The lever is repetition. Across the five pilot bugs, there were forty-one completed calls. Removing tokens from the stable system prefix creates repeated token-saving opportunities, and may reduce instruction conflict.

scenes.title: Lower-bound savings model
scenes.caption: Uses 41 completed calls and $3-$5 per million normal input tokens. Actual savings must come from A/B traces.

scenes.title: The decisive A/B matrix

cells: old-append-default at 822f3584 vs focused-replace at current main
one fixed metered profile first; Codex second pass after cleanup
Kitsoki pipeline only until prompt-variant result is known

scenes.caption: Same bug, same model, same hidden oracle, same guidance policy.

scenes.title: Research-grade success criteria

cells: all failures, adjudications, traces, and price assumptions included

scenes.caption: A cheaper prompt that causes quality regression should fail the study.

scenes.lede: Spend only after the fixtures are armed.
scenes.body: First verify query-string and kitsoki RED-at-baseline / GREEN-at-real-fix with bench.py. Then use drive_cell.sh --no-drive to print prompts for review. Only drive the 10 live cells after operator approval, because drive_cell.sh is the cost-bearing step.
scenes.narration: Before spending, verify the fixtures. Then print prompts with no-drive. Only after that should the operator approve the ten live cells, because drive-cell is the cost-bearing step.

scenes.lede: The honest story is stronger than the premature headline.
scenes.body: Existing traces justify the A/B: prompt overhead repeats across 41 completed calls, current costs are dominated by repeated context, and bug9 / bug14 show room for prompt-focus gains. The deck should claim readiness to test, not proven savings, until the 10 cells exist.
scenes.narration: The honest story is stronger than a premature headline. Existing traces justify the A/B. They do not yet prove savings. The next artifact should be the ten-cell matrix.
